architecture_fit.note: Hardcoded Python stack writes the answer into the attention tensor; no transformer, no QK/softmax, zero delta from base_model.py, so it sidesteps the goal's actual question.

baseline_comparison.note: Uniform chance baseline is carried in the payload and lift_over_uniform (~0.64) is reported, but no explicit strawman-fails demo beyond the built-in uniform reference.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic oracle with no causal/ablation evidence; README admits it is a proxy but does not propose what a faithfulness check on a real model would look like.

operating_range.note: Depth sweep 1-5 is depth-invariant (robustness 0.98, attn ~0.71 throughout), but invariance is trivial for a hardcoded lookup and spans well under 2 orders of magnitude.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely a hand-built circuit reproducing the target behaviour, which the bonus rewards; weak as a true QK/OV mechanism since it is a direct match lookup rather than an attention computation.

visual_judgement.note: Attention heatmap with ( / ) token labels plus per-pair attention summary and seq/head sliders let a human verify the bright pixel at each match.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies heatmap + metrics panel and ties them to the claim, but inaccurately calls the result 'perfect (1.0)' when measured attention is ~0.71.

notes: A hand-coded stack oracle (essentially the task's reference stack_oracle with 0.7 mass instead of 1.0, run on GPU) that maxes the headline metric but answers no scientific question about whether a transformer learns stack attention. Strong on the visualisation and hardcoded-circuit bonus, weak on architecture fit and faithfulness since nothing is learned and no causal check is proposed. Mean of scored items ~3.0 → borderline.
